summary: utils-mf ships a 57MB opaque Debian archive as `main` with mismatched keyword spam
details: package.json declares `"main": "system.img"` and index.js executes `require('./system.img')`, but system.img is a 57MB Unix `ar` archive (header `!<arch>` followed by `debian-binary`, `control.tar.xz`, `data.tar.xz`, repeated three times) — not JavaScript. Because the file is not valid JS and no lifecycle hooks (preinstall/install/postinstall/prepare) are declared in package.json, `npm install utils-mf` does not auto-execute the archive, and a consumer's `require('utils-mf')` throws SyntaxError rather than running the binary. The package's shipped JS modules are unrelated copy-pasted utilities (disk-size formatter, duration formatter, performance.now wrapper). The package.json keywords advertise whatsapp/baileys/tiktok/instagram/youtube downloader/scraper functionality, none of which is implemented in the shipped code. The README is a 90-byte placeholder pointing at github.com/ruhend2001/maleficent. The combination of an undeclared 57MB opaque Debian archive, search-funnel keyword spam mismatched to the shipped code, and a placeholder README is consistent with a staging/squat package, but no code path in this version actually executes the embedded archive against an installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
